Home
PFAS in My Community Federal Regulations State Legislation
← All states
Louisiana drinking-water testing

7 of 268 Louisiana water systems reported PFAS above the EPA standards Biden set in 2024.

3% of public water systems in Louisiana that EPA sampled for PFAS reported at least one result above the standards Biden's EPA finalized in 2024 for PFOA, PFOS, PFHxS, PFNA, or HFPO-DA (GenX). The Trump EPA is now moving to rescind those standards for every PFAS except PFOA and PFOS.

268
Public water systems tested
7
Exceeded an EPA standard
3%
Share of systems over limit
2%
Systems over PFOA limit
3%
Systems over PFOS limit
Showing all 268 systems
Water system PFAS over the EPA limit

What is Louisiana doing about PFAS?

As the federal drinking-water standards retreat, states are filling the gap with their own restrictions on PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, firefighting foam, and industrial discharges.

See Louisiana's PFAS legislation →

Source: EPA Fifth Unregulated Contaminant Monitoring Rule (UCMR 5). "Worst exceedance" shows how many times above the EPA standard the system's highest sample registered, and which compound it was. Biden's 2024 rule set limits of 4 ppt for PFOA and PFOS and 10 ppt for PFHxS, PFNA, and HFPO-DA (GenX). The Trump EPA proposed rescinding the latter three in May 2025.

Regulatory data: regulations.gov & Federal Register API. Press releases: EPA PFAS newsroom. Water-system PFAS testing: EPA UCMR 5 occurrence data. PFAS statistics: EWG, CDC NHANES. State legislation: Safer States.

Data last updated: run make fetch-all to update
